name: Viachaslau Pratasenia
subtitle: I build apps with Flutter, and I lead the team that ships them.

about_me.title: Flutter developer and Mobile Team Lead. 7+ years in.
about_me.text:
I came into Flutter from Android. That's still how I think about apps: they should be fast, and they shouldn't make people guess. With Flutter I get to ship one codebase to Android, iOS, web and desktop, which I still find pretty great.

These days I lead a small mobile team at Aventus IT. Half the job isn't code at all — planning, one-on-ones, reviews, unblocking people. But I haven't stopped writing code, and I don't want to. It keeps me honest about what the team is dealing with.

AI is just part of how I work now. I lean on AI assistants every day for the tedious stuff, and I wire up n8n with AI agents to automate the things none of us want to do twice. Nothing fancy — it just saves real time.

Mostly I want the apps we ship to be easy to live with, and the team behind them to not be miserable. That's it, really.

contact_me.title: Not really looking right now, but the inbox is open.
contact_me.text: Got something to ask, or just want to say hi? Write me. I actually read these, and I'll get back to you.

work.title: Flutter Developer / Mobile Team Lead
work.description:
I lead a team of five — three devs and two QA — on an e-commerce app, and I also build 7VPN. We ship to iOS, macOS, Windows and Web.

The lead side is calls, one-on-ones, standups, retros, planning, docs — the stuff that keeps a team moving. The hands-on side: I built the personal account website, social login and the news feature, and set up deep links, push notifications and the build environments everyone runs on.

Lately I've put real effort into end-to-end testing with Patrol — it walks through the app like a user would, which catches things unit tests just don't. I also use AI assistants for the day-to-day coding, and I've been playing with n8n and AI agents to see what's worth automating around the team.

Tools: Xcode, Android Studio, Figma, Firebase, Google Cloud Platform, Patrol, n8n.

work.title: Flutter Developer
work.description:
Two products at Symfa: a dating app and a corporate chat, basically our own Slack. The work was all over the place — adding Redux, migrating to null safety, chasing new Flutter releases.

Publishing was on me too. Whenever Google Play or the App Store flagged something, I was the one emailing their review teams back and forth until it cleared and users got a fix.

Tools: Xcode, Android Studio, Figma, Firebase.

work.title: Android/Flutter Developer
work.description:
Android/Flutter dev at Heapix, on a real mix of projects — food delivery, smart home control, a few smaller ones. Some I started from an empty repo, which I learned a lot from.

Honestly, working next to design and management taught me as much as the code did — how to talk to clients, scope things so they're actually doable, and a fair bit of project management I picked up by doing it.

Tools: Android Studio, Figma, Firebase, Adobe Illustrator.

projects.title: Personal Website
projects.description: This site — Flutter, on GitHub Pages. I kept it simple on purpose. The whole source is public, so if you're curious how it works, go poke around. It's also a fair bit of proof that Flutter does fine on the web.

projects.title: Never Have I Ever
projects.description: The party game, but on your phone. 10+ categories, 2000+ questions, good for a night with friends. Built in Flutter, shipped on Google Play.

projects.title: Workout Tracker App Design
projects.description: A workout tracker I designed in Figma. The idea was speed: log a set, check your progress, done. No clutter, nothing in the way.

blog_posts.title: The Easiest Way to Use Environments in Flutter
blog_posts.description: How to handle environment variables in Flutter without pulling in extra libraries — keeping config out of your code and easy to change.

blog_posts.title: Styling Flutter Apps Made Easy with Google Fonts
blog_posts.description: A quick walkthrough of using Google Fonts in Flutter — picking and swapping fonts with almost no effort to give your app a bit more character.

blog_posts.title: Flutter Version Management: A Comprehensive Guide
blog_posts.description: A practical guide to FVM for juggling multiple Flutter SDKs — installing versions, switching between them, and keeping projects consistent.
